element.label: div
element.text: 神隠しKamikakushiDark. Straw against your cheek, the smell of w

logTail.text: You rake the spilled rice back toward the basket. (+2 rice)
You rest against the post. The ache dulls; the light through the slats shifts.
You rake the spilled rice back toward the basket. (+2 rice)
You rest against the post. The ache dulls; the light through the slats shifts.
You rake the spilled rice back toward the basket. (+2 rice)
You rest against the post. The ache dulls; the light through the slats shifts.
You rake the spilled rice back toward the basket. (+2 rice)
Dawn at the gate. The forecourt is swept clean — your doing — and Genemon waits by the posts, watching you the way a man watches weather.
"You cleared the stores without being told twice. The house is short of hands and shorter of trustworthy ones. Stay — you're no day-hire now. Earn your rice."
logTail.speaker: Genemon

logTail.text: "The gate and the swept forecourt are yours to work now; stores come and go here. The home paddies too — the rice that feeds the house. And the kura's own repair is yours to tend: spend the house's small surplus to shore it up."
logTail.speaker: Genemon

logTail.text: A pack-laden stranger has laid a mat in the lee of the gate-post — an Ōmi pedlar, come because a tended gate draws trade. He lifts a hand as you pass.
"A tended gate's a lucky gate for a man with a pack. Tokubei, of Ōmi — mind if I keep my mat here a while, young master? Coin of your own spends as well as any lord's."
logTail.speaker: Tokubei

logTail.text: "I mean to rise past a kept hand."
logTail.speaker: You

logTail.text: "...Ambition, in a hand kept a day. Mind it doesn't outrun your worth."
logTail.speaker: Genemon

logTail.text: The gate and the swept forecourt are yours to work now — stores come and go here.
The terraced home paddies open to you — the rice that feeds the house.
You begin to mark the turning of the days, and the four seasons with them.
Rank ↑ — Kept hand 下人
The estate's own state of repair is yours to tend now — spend the house's surplus to shore it up.
The rice you clear and grow is food, not only trade — a plain bowl of it fills a hollow belly and puts you back to work the faster.
